headline: Treasuries Fall as Trump’s Iran Threats Add to Inflation Concern
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Treasuries dropped after President Donald Trump’s threatening tone toward Iran in a prime-time address pushed up oil prices and added to concern over inflation.US two-year yields climbed as much as six basis points to 3.86%, while those on the 10-year rose as high as 4.38%. The dollar strengthened against all its Group-of-10 peers.Hopes for a quick end to the Middle East conflict were dashed after Trump said the US plans to launch fresh attacks on Iran within the next two to three

headline: After the drop, is Reddit a buy or a risk? BofA weighs in
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America remains Neutral on Reddit shares despite the social media platform's year-to-date pullback, arguing that while the company has genuine long-term growth drivers, its premium valuation and macro sensitivity make it difficult to turn more constructive right now.

headline: BofA cuts India growth forecast, flags rising inflation risks
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America has cut its Indian growth forecast and raised its inflation projections, warning that a prolonged energy price shock from the Middle East conflict is beginning to leave visible marks on the country’s economy.

headline: Citi Tells Some Staff to Work From Home After Foiled Bomb Plot at BofA in Paris
summary: Citigroup has asked its employees in Paris and Frankfurt to work from home as a precautionary measure following a foiled bomb plot last week against a Bank of America office in the French capital.  “The safety of our employees is our top priority, and we are taking the necessary measures to ensure their protection,” a Citi spokeswoman said.  “Employees in Paris and Frankfurt are working remotely as a precautionary measure,” she added without specifying how long the measure would be in place.

headline: BoE to hike before cutting, says BofA as energy shock persists
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America now expects the Bank of England to raise interest rates twice before pivoting to cuts, as high energy prices raise the risk of second-round inflation effects feeding through to the broader U.K. economy.

headline: How The Amplitude (AMPL) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Guidance And Valuation Resets
summary: Amplitude’s blended fair value estimate shifted from $15.67 to $12.70, about a 19% reset that has sharpened investor focus on where the stock should trade today. This move lines up with recent Street research, where several firms cut price targets while debating how to balance solid revenue execution with more cautious margin and valuation assumptions. As you read on, you will see how to track these changing signals and what they may mean for your own view on Amplitude. Analyst Price Targets...

headline: How The Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) Investment Story Is Shifting With AI And Valuation Resets
summary: Cadence Design Systems is working through a refined analyst view, with the modeled fair value shifting from US$384.20 to US$371.68 as part of the latest update. Analysts link this change to sector wide software volatility. At the same time, they point to execution on earnings, recurring software demand tied to AI, and raised long term EPS estimates as support for the story. As you read on, you will see how these moving pieces shape the evolving market narrative around Cadence and what to...

headline: How The Torrid Holdings (CURV) Story Is Shifting With A New Fair Value Target
summary: The latest update on Torrid Holdings centers on a refreshed price target, with the assessed fair value per share moving from US$1.475 to US$1.58. Street research around this new US$1.58 mark highlights a split view, with some analysts pointing to potential upside and others stressing execution risks tied to the assumptions behind that target. Read on to see what is driving this evolving narrative and how you can keep track of the key moving parts. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture...
